$comment: Generative news-article corpus for the 2010s-and-beyond (hybrid / social-media / live-blog voice: fast, present-tense, second-screen, power units and DRS and telemetry everywhere), parsed by Companion.Core.News.NewsArticleBank. Keys under 'bodies' are 'journalPhase|cause'; each holds era-keyed body templates selected deterministically via the 'headlines' PCG32 stream. Bodies compose literal prose, fact tokens, and {pool:name} references into 'pools'. Fact tokens: {player} {team} {race} {year} {round} {position} {expected} {winner} {fieldSize} {champPosition} {champMove} {champLeader}.

2010s: LIVE FROM {race} — {year}. Flag's out, data's in.
{race}, {year}: timesheets final, telemetry logged, takes already flying.
Lights out to checkered at {race} — here's how it landed.
Trackside at {race}, {year}. Reaction coming in fast.
Race report, {race} {year}, as it happened.
The pit wall is grinning and the timing screens agree.
Cue the paddock buzz — this one trends.
Momentum, and every dashboard in the paddock backs it up.
The garage erupts and the analysts are already replaying it.
A result that reads well on every screen. On to the next round.
default: A strong day at the office.

2010s: The debrief will be a long one, telemetry open on every monitor.
The engineers go quiet and the data gets pulled apart.
One to delete from the memory banks — reset for the next round.
Not what the numbers promised. Straight back to the simulator.
The paddock chatter turns critical; the team just wants next weekend.
default: The team resets for the next round.

2010s: Standings update: {player} sits {champPosition}, {champMove}, with {champLeader} setting the pace at the top.
The title picture reads {player} {champPosition} and {champMove}, chasing {champLeader}.
That leaves {player} {champPosition} in the championship, {champMove}.
Championship-wise: {player} {champPosition}, {champMove}.
from a car the models had pegged at {expected}
against a data-driven forecast of {expected}
with the pre-race sims calling {expected} at best
DRS deployed, tyres managed, the strategy call nailed.
Power unit deployment on point all afternoon.
The undercut worked and the pit wall read the race to the lap.
Tyre deg managed, energy harvest maxed, every sector backing the plan.
default: A clean run on setup and strategy.

2010s: The commentary booth calls it the drive of the day.
Second-screen verdict: nailed it.
The analysts are already queuing up to praise it.
The feeds light up and the paddock consensus forms fast — glowing.
default: A drive the analysts will replay.

2010s: {pool:dateline} {player} wins {race}. Lights to flag, {team} in total control of a {fieldSize}-car field, and nobody laid a glove on them. {pool:techNote} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} Win in the bag for {player} — {team} manage the gap, hit every window, and take {race} going away. {pool:punditTake} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} P1 for {player} at {race}. The {team} car had the legs all weekend and the timing screens never lied, {pool:expectationBeat}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
default: {player} wins {race} for {team}, leading home a {fieldSize}-car field. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

2010s: {pool:dateline} Podium for {player} — {position} at {race}, splitting the big names as {winner} takes the win. {team} banked it with a smart strategy call. {pool:techNote} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} {player} climbs the rostrum at {race}, home {position} behind {winner} in a {fieldSize}-car field. {pool:punditTake} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} Champagne for {team}. {player} brings it home {position} at {race}, a drive {pool:expectationBeat}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} Points on the board for {player} — {position} at {race} from a {fieldSize}-car field, {winner} up front. Not a headline day, but the tally moves. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} {team} bank the points at {race}, {player} home {position}. The strategy call was solid and the score ticks over. {pool:techNote} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} Drive of the day from {player}. {position} at {race} {pool:expectationBeat} — the {team} garage can't quite believe the timesheet. {pool:punditTake} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} {player} punches way above the car at {race}, hauling {team} to {position} in a {fieldSize}-car field {pool:expectationBeat}. {pool:techNote} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} Off the pace for {player} — {position} at {race} when the sims called {expected}. {team} will pull the data apart, and {winner} led it. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} A flat afternoon for {team}. {player} home only {position} in a {fieldSize}-car field, well short of the {expected} the form book wanted. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}
default: {player} underdelivers at {race}, only {position} when {expected} was on the cards. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}

2010s: {pool:dateline} Power unit gremlins end {player}'s race at {race}. The {team} car cries enough while running strong — no fault of the driver — and {winner} takes the win. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} Smoke on the telemetry and it's over for {player} at {race}, a mechanical failure with {expected} still on the table. The garage goes silent. {pool:closeBad}
default: {player} retires from {race} with a mechanical failure. {pool:closeBad}

2010s: {pool:dateline} {player} into the barriers at {race}. A moment's misjudgement and the run is over, leaving {team} to count the cost while {winner} leads home a {fieldSize}-car field. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} A costly error from {player} at {race} — the weekend thrown away by the driver's own hand with {expected} in reach. The debrief will sting. {pool:closeBad}
default: {player} crashes out of {race} through driver error. {pool:closeBad}

2010s: {pool:dateline} A quiet run for {player}, {position} at {race} in a {fieldSize}-car field. Nothing flashy — laps logged, car home, data banked. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} No headlines for {team} at {race}. {player} brings it home {position}, {winner} up front, and the weekend goes in the books. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
default: {player} runs to {position} at {race}, a quiet day for {team}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
